title: Bridge Essay: Auditory verbal hallucinations: The ins and outs of subtypes.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

**Auditory verbal hallucinations: The ins and outs of subtypes.**. Psychiatry Res.. PMID: 40840195. DOI: 10.1016/j.psychres.2025.116696. BACKGROUND: Voice-hearing in psychosis is aheterogeneous and often distressing experience. Phenomenological studies have proposed the existence of several subtypes of auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) that may guide intervention, such as inner-speech based voices (ISVs), hypervigilance voices (HVs) and memory-based voices, but empirical data is scarce. We developed adetailed coding scheme with the aim of examining whether distinct subtypes of AVHs could be reliably identified and character
